methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Valthos is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, supplemented by reference to 2 public defense-tech names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 additional private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Tenna Systems.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage, implying $75M. Peer-median analysis references the 5-company comp set in Defense & Security, which ranges from $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A), with a median valuation of $300M. This wide dispersion reflects Stage A heterogeneity within the sector. The peer-median method implies $300M.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $64M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $188M. The $64M floor reflects a conservative stage-progression view. The $345M ceiling reflects weighting toward upper-quartile peers. Given the 5.4x spread between methods and the material dispersion within the peer set, key uncertainties include: traction and technical differentiation relative to mature Series A competitors, team track record in defense applications, and customer validation. A valuation in the $100M–$200M range appears most defensible absent additional proprietary performance data.
